name: Honey Baked Squash
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 cups Quaker Oats, uncooked (quick or old-fashioned)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Wash the squash and cut it in half crosswise. Remove seeds and membranes.
3. Cut each half into 1/2-inch thick slices.
4. Arrange the slices in a 11x7-inch glass baking dish.
5. Drizzle with margarine.
6. Bake for 30 minutes.
7. Sprinkle with Add-A-Crunch.
8. Bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until the squash is tender.
ADD-A-CRUNCH:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Grease a 15x10-inch jelly roll pan.
3. In a medium bowl, combine all Add-A-Crunch ingredients and mix well.
4. Spread into the prepared pan.
5. Bake for 18 to 20 minutes, or until light golden brown, stirring once.
6. Spread onto an ungreased cookie sheet; cool thoroughly.
7. Set aside 1 cup, refrigerate unused portion, tightly covered, up to 3 months.
8. Serve as a cereal or on fruit or ice cream.
Microwave Directions:
1. In a large microwaveable bowl, combine Add-A-Crunch ingredients as directed.
2. Microwave at HIGH for 5 to 7 minutes, or until golden, stirring 3 times.
3. Spread onto an ungreased cookie sheet; cool and store as directed.
4. Slice the squash as directed and arrange in a 11x7-inch glass baking dish.
5. Cover with vented plastic wrap.
6. Microwave at HIGH for 7 to 10 minutes, or until tender, rotating the dish once.
7. Drizzle with margarine; sprinkle with Add-A-Crunch.
8. Microwave at HIGH for an additional 1 to 2 minutes.
